textByHash.0b203e45273375172407c19fad6fac0737583c66: These rebels had better get a move on and defeat Lord Iorwerth. Old ladies like me have a tendency to drop dead, and I'll be very annoyed if that happens before I see Prifddinas again.
textByHash.0d92f9c550849c22b94a7a11361566f5ded9a42c: Tell me human, where is Baxtorian?
textByHash.2a277ca510aae0efc90fc134ed96688936ad3c7e: Who has awoken me? A human? You are not who I expected.
textByHash.3a87be0aeaf148e938e6906d54552a1c2320b925: Boy, what did I tell you about insulting the human!
textByHash.4d82202e0f9607eb33e0497bc0be4c9398a0e384: I don't know this human here, but I damn well don't know you either. The human was capable enough to find me and fix my exoskeleton. Maybe they are capable of this as well.
textByHash.53c34df77825cbbd2a16e1461e71d0b9045141b0: Baxtorian lives? And he is working with a human? This is most irregular. I see you repaired my exoskeleton as well. Impressive work for a human, but again, most irregular.
textByHash.5bd188043edaa651802783a34bbc1f75568c161b: Oh yes, I'm the oldest elf still alive. You can thank this exoskeleton for that, one of our finer inventions.
textByHash.608afd9b815dac2264693839f336dc47f42a9685: Then I will journey there at once. There is clearly a lot I need to catch up on.
textByHash.68389bed600f72ddc4c575775979ea265e90c9c8: Most of the elves around here give me a wide berth. They seem to find me intimidating. How ironic that an old woman is more of a threat to them than the entire Iorwerth clan.
textByHash.75209ce66be084312c579a1a4300dde93998d272: I've lived for thousands of years. If I'm going to die today, I'd rather just get on with it.
textByHash.9296389889fd4298fc3afe5f09ae2de6a6119246: Were you there when Seren made those seals, boy?
textByHash.a88c37db49640f9c9ccb24c215cdb3544e43c47b: Good to see you . You're very brave to come see me.
textByHash.aa1c080606e3f71037a5d3f44e468f93de985b43: I'd personally recommend the one about Zalcano, a fun tale about how we trapped her beneath the city. She's still here now in fact, feel free to go give her a poke.
textByHash.b151d0e93895b17130955ea4cfe23e22fc8381fa: Yes, but not the time to tell them. They have plenty of books in the Tower of Voices if you want stories, most of them are from our Grand Library.
textByHash.bfd1ab06c812d008b6c5666556efe11c81cf3d3b: Lord Iorwerth better show up soon! I can't wait forever!
textByHash.ceaab9b6f6b0798bea7f03b9a753e1e5f45d0685: Then how do you know what our goddess intended?
textByHash.e685bf2a1ae5dac79eca75ace9ee065f0d7ee0cc: Oh I'll cook up quite the storm in the afterlife, believe me.
